cards.front: Inner product of two vectors u and v
cards.back: The inner product of two vectors u and v is denoted by (u|v) and is a scalar value that satisfies certain properties, including linearity, positive definiteness, and conjugate symmetry.
A set of vectors is said to be orthogonal if the inner product of any two distinct vectors in the set is zero. In other words, for a set {u1, u2, ..., un}, (ui|uj) = 0 for all i ≠ j.
The length of a vector u, denoted by ||u|| or ||u||1, is the square root of the inner product of u with itself: ||u|| = sqrt((u|u)).

The Gram-Schmidt process is an algorithm for orthonormalizing a set of vectors. It involves iteratively subtracting the projection of a vector onto a previous vector in the set, resulting in an orthonormal set.

QR-factorization is a factorization technique that decomposes a matrix A into the product of an orthogonal matrix Q and an upper triangular matrix R. It is useful for solving systems of linear equations and least squares problems.
